dependencyDashboardTitle: Renovate Dashboard â€” #902 L3 third-party image digests

customManagers.description: #902 L3 â€” split-knob Helm values (image.repository/tag/digest) for the 7 digest-pinned third-party charts. The '# renovate:' annotation above each 'repository:' anchors the match so multi-image charts (da-portal/tenant-api also carry a first-party app image) cannot cross-match tag/digest across blocks.

#902 L3 â€” single-line 'image: repo:tag@sha256:...' refs: mariadb-instance Helm values, raw k8s manifests, and the tenant-api alpine/git init template. Only refs that already carry an @sha256 digest match â€” first-party tag-only images (handled by the release flow) are skipped.

#902 L3 â€” the scan-thirdparty matrix 'ref:' entries in nightly-image-scan.yaml. Renovate updates these in the SAME branch as the deploy ref (identical depName) so the drift-guard test (matrix == deploy refs) stays green; a missed sync turns the drift-guard RED in the Renovate PR's own CI.

packageRules.description: #902 L3 â€” group all non-major third-party image updates (incl. digest-only refreshes) into ONE PR for low review noise. The self-hosted workflow's weekly cron sets the cadence; a manual workflow_dispatch (incl. dry-run) runs on demand.

#902 L3 â€” MAJOR upgrades can break (grafana/prometheus/mariadb majors carry config/schema changes). Require an explicit tick on the Dependency Dashboard before Renovate opens the PR, rather than auto-flooding a breaking bump.

#902 L3 â€” `python:3.x-slim` numbers its tag by Python FEATURE release, so a 3.13â†’3.14 bump is semver-MINOR to Docker but a real language-version jump (new interpreter, possible C-ABI / stdlib breakage for dependent components). Gate python's MINOR bumps behind the Dependency Dashboard like a major, rather than letting them auto-flow in the grouped PR. Digest-only refreshes (same tag, security re-pin) still auto-flow â€” only the version bump waits for a tick.
